name: Stainless Steel Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Stainless Steel. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Stainless Steel oxide layer removal

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength optimized for Stainless Steel absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with minimal heat accumulation

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive processing

dwellTime.description: Effective dwell time per spot for contaminant ablation

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm fiber lasers in industrial cleaning of stainless steel to remove contaminants without melting; starting at 200 W for balanced efficiency and safety. Max limited to avoid thermal damage given SS reflectivity (~60% at 1064 nm). Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and study in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contamination removal; slower (500-1500 mm/s) for heavy rust/oil, faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light oxides. Recommended 2000 mm/s for optimal balance on SS to minimize heat input. Source: Industry standards from Laserax manuals and Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) case studies.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime typical for cost-effective industrial cleaning of SS; picosecond (0.001-1 ns equivalent) or femtosecond for ultra-low HAZ but less common. 50 ns recommended to reduce thermal effects while ablating contaminants. Source: Laser physics review in Applied Surface Science (2019), noting minimal HAZ at <100 ns for metals.

repetition_rate.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning; higher rates (>100 kHz) speed up process but risk heat accumulation on reflective SS. 50 kHz balances speed and thermal management. Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021), highlighting trade-offs for stainless steel.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe ablation of contaminants; SS damage threshold ~4 J/cm² for ns pulses at 1064 nm due to high thermal conductivity. 1.5 J/cm² recommended for effective cleaning without substrate pitting. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for cleaning; smaller (100-200 μm) for precision surface prep, larger (200-500 μm) for broad areas. 200 μm recommended for uniform SS cleaning to control fluence. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf lasers, per Optics Express (2022).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy layers to avoid excessive energy per pass on heat-sensitive SS. 3 passes recommended for complete removal with minimal distortion. Source: Industry case studies in Surface and Coatings Technology (2019).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or over-ablation; 50% optimal for SS to achieve even cleaning while preventing heat buildup. Source: Process optimization in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2020).
